active_projects.description: Construct D as the group of order-preserving automorphisms (translations) of the canonical timeline of MCSs, with a designated origin. D emerges from pure syntax via: consistent set → MCS origin (Lindenbaum) → linear order T of reachable MCSs (existence lemma) → D = Aut+(T) (translation group) → eval₀ : D ≅ T (origin bijection) → linearly ordered abelian group → task_rel = group action → canonical model → truth lemma → completeness. Supersedes DovetailingChain, SuccOrder, and hardcoded D=Int approaches.

active_projects.description: Implement sorry-free completeness via CanonicalMCS domain. Use CanonicalFMCS.lean infrastructure (sorry-free forward_F/backward_P) to prove standard completeness theorem. Infrastructure (Lindenbaum, existence lemma, CanonicalR, BidirectionalFragment) complete and sorry-free. DovetailingChain approach blocked by F-persistence; superseded by Task 956 (translation group construction).

artifacts.summary: Comprehensive analysis identifying fundamental obstacle: standard valid requires LinearOrderedAddCommGroup time, CanonicalMCS is only Preorder. Recommends verifying soundness for Preorder time as primary path, with DovetailingChain sorry resolution as fallback.
Antisymmetrization approach: Z-indexed chain through CanonicalMCS via incremental dovetailing. Each F/P witness is independent Lindenbaum MCS. 35-60 hours, 55-70% success probability.
Direct linearization via linearity axioms. Key finding: FPClosure (countable closure under F/P witnesses) + Int embedding avoids F-persistence problem. 22-33 hours estimated.

Recursive unpacking analysis. Recommends Rat embedding via Mathlib Order.embedding_from_countable_to_dense (12-18 hours) over Int embedding (22-33 hours). BidirectionalFragment IS the constructed time domain.

Bundle automorphism analysis. Finding: fragment automorphism approach NOT viable - BidirectionalFragment has trivial automorphism group, no natural AddCommGroup on quotient. MF/TF soundness requires AddCommGroup. Rat embedding from research-005 remains recommended.

Comprehensive comparison: Approach A (change valid) requires 40-60h, breaks MF/TF soundness. Approach B (FPClosure + Int embedding via orderIsoIntOfLinearSuccPredArch) requires 15-20h, preserves all proofs. Key insight: discreteness axiom is self-contradictory with current valid definition. Approach B strongly recommended.

Team research (2 teammates): MCS subsets as times + history functions. Finding: BidirectionalFragment IS the temporal structure, FMCS IS the history function. Enriched chain omega-squared diagonal fix is the primary path. 3 sorries identified with resolution paths.
Deep analysis of time-shift groups, torsor constructions, and frame-first completeness; confirms embedding into Int via enriched chain as the narrowest path to sorry-free completeness
Intrinsic group construction via Successor Algebra on BidirectionalQuotient, representation theorem extensible by discreteness/density axioms
Frame construction from pure syntax: durations as Antisymmetrization of BidirectionalFragment, AddCommGroup via Successor Algebra (orderIsoIntOfLinearSuccPredArch)

Team research (3 teammates): G/H symmetry confirmed via duality bridges, Preorder vs AddCommGroup two-layer architecture mapped, disconnected chains resolved by BidirectionalFragment. Full syntactic D construction outlined.

Analysis of AddCommGroup construction on D without discreteness/density assumptions. Proves no neutral group exists (Archimedean dichotomy), canonical model is inherently discrete, D=Int is correct for completeness.

Definitive sorry-free completeness construction: BidirectionalFragment IS the canonical domain (proven LinearOrder), fragmentFMCS is sorry-free, compose Int enumeration with fragment FMCS to sidestep F-persistence. Key obligation: SuccOrder coverness on BidirectionalQuotient.

SuccOrder approach blocked: coverness unprovable (Lindenbaum adds G-formulas), NoMaxOrder fails (reflexive semantics). 14 lemmas added. Recommends Option A (generalize TaskFrame) or Option B (direct chain with fragmentFSucc).
Phase 1 partial: 14 lemmas added (quotientSucc/Pred, GContent/HContent equivalence, mcs_has_F/P_top). SuccOrder coverness blocked. Plan revision needed.
Team research (3 teammates): Root-cause analysis of Phase 1 blockers. Architectural mismatch confirmed. Two-phase completeness (BFMCS Preorder + transfer to TaskFrame) recommended.

Deep math research: Single elegant architecture via trivial task frame trick. AddCommGroup not needed for completeness. BFMCS falsification transfers to standard valid via trivial task_rel. Frame correspondence becomes corollary, not prerequisite.

Minimal TaskFrame parametrization: two-level hierarchy (TemporalFrame [LinearOrder D] extends to TaskFrame [AddCommGroup D]). Trivial task frame embedding theorem. Frame correspondence: discreteness axiom <-> SuccOrder <-> Int, density axiom <-> DenselyOrdered <-> Rat.

Non-trivial task_rel from pure syntax. Chain-based construction: task_rel w d u := exists chain position i with chain(i)=w and chain(i+d)=u. Requires chain injectivity. D=Int acceptable but BidirectionalQuotient lacks AddCommGroup. Key risk: chain stalling (quotientSucc fixed point). Trivial task_rel approach BANNED.

Phase 1 blocker: quotientSucc/quotientPred inverse is mathematically impossible. G(phi)->H(phi) semantically invalid (countermodel: 3-point linear order). Recommends chain-based task_rel approach from research-018 Section 8.
Parametric completeness via zsmul embedding: D requires [Nontrivial D] [Archimedean D]. Step-function histories preserve truth. Transfer from Int to arbitrary D.

Team research 021 (3 Opus teammates): maps ideals, catalogs 20 reports, identifies 5 critical gaps and 4 alternative paths. Key findings: transfer theorem unverified, fragment density uninvestigated, trivial task_rel is mathematically natural, two-layer refactor is architecturally correct.

Teammate D (follow-on): answered all 5 gating questions from source. Confirmed: transfer theorem already proven (canonical_truth_lemma_all), ShiftClosed already resolved, fragment is discrete (D=Q blocked), all families need TC (not just eval), trivial task_rel is correct. Root cause: DovetailingChain forward_F/backward_P sorries.
Deep analysis of histories-first approach: R-order properties, Flag infrastructure, fragment-level completeness strategy, and concrete path to zero sorries

Deep analysis of alternative encoding strategies for F-persistence. Pre-saturation fails (multi-witness seed inconsistency), bounded jumps impossible, saturation-first collapses to constant families. Recommends fragment-level completeness + order-theoretic SuccOrder for Int embedding.

active_projects.description:
Meta-analysis of 16 research reports and 12 plan versions to extract actionable insights for overcoming the forward_F/backward_P completeness challenge. CONSTRAINT: Sorry debt is NEVER acceptable — the study must find a path to zero sorries, not evaluate sorry acceptance as an option.

The study should focus on LEARNING FROM FAILURES to inspire creative solutions:

**Pattern Analysis of Failed Approaches:**
- What common assumptions did linear chains, enriched chains, omega-squared chains, witness-graph-guided approaches, and FPreservingSeed all share that led to failure?
- Is the "Lindenbaum opacity" a fundamental barrier, or does it reveal a hidden assumption we can drop?
- Why did 12 successive plan revisions each believe their approach would work when all hit the same wall? What cognitive trap is at play?

**Creative Reframing Questions:**
- What if the problem is not "prove forward_F for a chain" but "find a structure that makes forward_F trivially true"?
- Are there proof techniques from other domains (set theory, category theory, type theory) that handle similar "witness existence under opacity" problems?
- Can we work backward from the goal state (a BFMCS Int with forward_F) to discover what properties the construction MUST have, rather than forward from chain construction?
- What if we abandon BFMCS Int entirely and prove completeness through a different semantic structure?

**Mandatory Outputs:**
1. A "lessons learned" document identifying the precise mathematical property that all failed approaches lacked
2. At least 3 genuinely novel approach ideas that do NOT fit the "bottom-up chain construction" pattern
3. For each novel approach: a preliminary feasibility assessment with confidence level
4. A ranked recommendation with clear decision criteria

Key artifacts to study: `specs/916_implement_fp_witness_obligation_tracking/` (all reports, plans, handoffs), particularly the Phase 3 blocker analysis (`handoffs/phase-3-blocker-analysis-20260224.md`) which crystallizes the obstruction.

active_projects.description: Prove the canonical model density frame condition under irreflexive semantics from temporal axioms alone, without importing any external dense linear order. The condition: for all MCS M, M prime with CanonicalR M M_prime and NOT CanonicalR M_prime M, there exists W with CanonicalR M W AND CanonicalR W M_prime. This is the blocker for task 956 Phase 5 (staged_timeline_densely_ordered). Two sub-cases: Case A (G(delta) not in M) has a direct proof via seed GContent(M) union {neg delta}; Case B (G(delta) in M, delta not in M) remains open - investigate whether axioms force a Case A reduction or provide an alternative intermediate construction. Key constraint: must derive density purely from temporal axioms (linearity, density F(phi)->F(F(phi)), seriality); importing Q or any external dense linear order is FORBIDDEN as it violates the D-from-syntax requirement of task 956.

active_projects.completion_summary: Proved the density frame condition for the canonical model under irreflexive temporal semantics. Phase 4 eliminated the sorry in IRR soundness by restricting to domain-inhabited evaluation times. Phase 5 resolved Case B of density_frame_condition using a novel purely syntactic argument: when G(delta) is in M but delta is not in M, either M' is reflexive (take W = M') or there exists an alternative distinguishing formula from GContent(M') that falls into Case A. Two sorries eliminated across two files.
